channel.title: Dissensus — Research Updates
channel.description: Research updates from Dissensus — formalising productive friction in multi-agent systems.

item.title: Dissensus Ltd: the lab has a legal shape
item.description: Dissensus is incorporated as a private limited company in England and Wales; the research stays open.

item.title: The Trident: three ways a claim fails, counted properly
item.description: A pocket-sized diagnostic for single claims, a Lean 4 core that checks the count, and a framework that failed its own test and printed the repair.

item.title: The Born Rule, Demodulated
item.description: Notes on a speculative preprint: measurement as finite-window demodulation plus threshold detection, and the square in |ψ|² as the signature of an energy detector.

item.title: Paper Archive with Google Scholar Metadata
item.description: Individual paper pages now available with Highwire Press, Dublin Core, and JSON-LD metadata for Google Scholar indexing. 26 papers across all ASCRI programmes.

item.title: The Replicator-Optimization Mechanism - arXiv Submission
item.description: A unified computational framework for self-replicating, optimization-executing systems across physical and abstract substrates. Submitted to arXiv and under review at Synthese.

item.title: Stakes Without Voice - New Working Paper
item.description: Structural exclusion and the legitimacy deficit problem. When stakeholders bear costs but lack representation in governance decisions.

item.title: Identity is Irreducibly Relational
item.description: Ontological foundations for relational functionalism. Why identity cannot be reduced to intrinsic properties.

item.title: Dissensus Research Lab Launched
item.description: Official launch of dissensus.ai - a governance alignment research lab. Home of the Adversarial Systems & Complexity Research Initiative (ASCRI).

item.title: The Doctrine of Consensual Sovereignty
item.description: Quantifying legitimacy in adversarial environments. Introduces stakes-weighted consent alignment as a formal measure of political legitimacy.

item.title: Dissolving Qualia via Occam's Razor
item.description: Eliminative monism and the computational basis of phenomenological illusion. A ~33,000 word monograph on consciousness.
